title: musa peekelii subsp. peekelii
intro: Musa peekelii subsp. peekelii, also known as the Peekel banana, is a species of wild banana native to New Guinea. It is a type of herbaceous plant that belongs to the Musaceae family, characterized by its large leaves and edible fruit. The Peekel banana is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote health and well-being, and to support the immune system and digestive health.
